title: EDPFY vs. EXC: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
text: Investors with an interest in Utility - Electric Power stocks have likely encountered both Energias de Portugal (EDPFY) and Exelon (EXC). But which of these two companies is the best option for those looking for undervalued stocks?

title: Exelon: Attractive Finances And Valuation Plus Resistance To Recessions
text: Exelon Corporation is a large regulated electric utility that serves four of the largest cities in the United States. The company is relatively resistant to economic shocks, which should be attractive if the economy falls into a recession during the second half of 2023. The company is positioned to deliver a 9.5% to 11.5% average annual total return through 2026.

title: Climate Change Investment Initiative Paves the Way for Innovation in Baltimore
text: CHICAGO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Startups whose products, services or technologies are focused on fighting climate change and improving the quality of life for residents of the Baltimore region and beyond, are encouraged to apply for an equity investment from 2c2i, a joint effort of the Exelon Foundation and Exelon Corporation (Nasdaq: EXC).
